meta.name: Envision Typography Tokens
meta.description: Source of truth for Envision Inc. typographic system. Sourced from the Envision Brand Book (2026 Edition). Three typefaces, each with a specific role. Never substitute.

primary-print.description: Primary typeface. Used for headlines and body copy in print and designed materials. Packed with confidence and clarity. Licensed font — requires Envision license for use.

primary-web.description: Web typeface. Used for headlines and body copy in all digital and HTML formats. Freely available via Google Fonts.

logo-only.description: Reserved exclusively for the Envision logo. Never use in any other context. Reserving this font for the logo strengthens brand identity and keeps the logo distinct.
forbidden-for: Any text other than the logo wordmark

print-fallback-stack.description: CSS fallback chain for print-intended PDFs or digital files that target Gotham. Gotham must be licensed and installed; Montserrat is the nearest acceptable substitute.

web-fallback-stack.description: CSS font-family stack for web. Montserrat must be loaded; the fallback chain exists for email clients and systems without webfonts.

email-fallback.description: Email-safe fallback. Use when Montserrat cannot render (Outlook desktop, some corporate email clients).

mono.description: Code, data tables, and monospace UI only. Not for brand-facing materials.

book.description: Gotham Book — body copy in print materials.

regular.description: Montserrat Regular — body copy in web materials.

bold.description: Headlines, subheads, buttons. Brand book default for headlines.

black.description: High-impact display. Use sparingly for hero moments.

display-xl.description: Hero display. Landing pages, large print.

body-lg.description: Large body for hero paragraphs and featured copy.

micro.description: Print legal copy only. Minimum legible size.

tight.description: Buttons and large subheads per brand book (100-110%).

body.description: Brand book spec: body copy leading 140-160%. Using 150% as default.

body-spacious.description: Long-form reading. Brand book spec top of range.

subhead-large.description: Brand book spec: large uppercase subheads tracking 0.5em. Wide airy treatment.

subhead-small.description: Brand book spec: small subheads tracking -0.02em.

body.description: Brand book spec: body copy 0 to -0.02em.

button.description: Brand book spec: buttons uppercase tracking 0.25em.

headline-print.description: Brand book headline spec — Gotham Bold or Black, title case, leading 110%, tracking -0.045em.

headline-web.description: Brand book headline spec for web — Montserrat Bold or Black, title case, leading 110%, tracking -0.045em.

subhead-large.description: Brand book large subhead — Montserrat Bold, uppercase, leading 100-110%, tracking 0.5em. Airy and confident.

subhead-small.description: Brand book small subhead — Montserrat Bold, leading 100-110%, tracking -0.02em.

body-print.description: Brand book body spec for print — Gotham Book, leading 140-160%, tracking 0 to -0.02em.

body-web.description: Brand book body spec for web — Montserrat Regular, leading 140-160%, tracking 0 to -0.02em.

button.description: Brand book button spec — Montserrat Bold, uppercase, leading 100-110%, tracking 0.25em.

allowed: Use Gotham for all Envision print and designed materials (requires license).
Use Montserrat for all web, HTML, and digital materials.
Apply exact brand book tracking: headlines -0.045em, buttons 0.25em uppercase, large subheads 0.5em uppercase.
Headlines are title case — not all-caps. All-caps is reserved for large uppercase subheads and buttons.
Use bold (700) as the default weight for headlines. Reserve black (900) for hero/display moments.
forbidden: Do not use Humanist for any purpose other than the Envision logo.
Do not substitute Gotham or Montserrat for a 'similar' font (no Poppins, Inter, Roboto, Lato, Helvetica as primary).
Do not use italic for emphasis — use weight 700+ instead.
Do not use all-caps for headlines or body copy.
Do not use em dashes anywhere in Envision copy (brand voice rule).
Do not combine more than three weights in a single layout.
